does: This paper presents AliyunConsoleAgent, a web agent framework designed to automatically verify that cloud platform documentation accurately reflects current user interfaces and executable workflows in real-world cloud consoles. The core contribution addresses the massive verification gap at cloud platforms like Alibaba Cloud, where hundreds of products with rapid feature iteration create systematic divergence between documented procedures and actual console interfaces. The authors propose a two-stage training approach: first, supervised fine-tuning on trajectories distilled from frontier proprietary models to initialize agent behavior, then reinforcement learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) paired with a dual-channel outcome reward model trained directly in actual cloud environments. The system includes infrastructure innovations like Terraform-based resource pre-provisioning and LLM-driven on-demand provisioning to enable high-determinism, large-scale RL training without prohibitive API costs.
connects: This work builds on established lines of research in web automation agents, imitation learning from demonstrations, and reinforcement learning for long-horizon tasks. It extends prior approaches to open-domain web navigation and API-driven environments by combining trajectory distillation from larger models—following the paradigm of knowledge distillation in large language models—with policy optimization directly in realistic, non-simulated environments. The dual-channel reward modeling approach connects to recent work on outcome-based reward specifications and multi-objective optimization in RL. The paper also addresses practical deployment constraints that have limited prior agent systems: the prohibitive costs of frontier model APIs and data privacy concerns that make cloud platforms reluctant to send console interactions to external services. By achieving this through smaller, fine-tuned models operating in real environments, it responds to the gap between high-performing proprietary agents and deployable systems.
enables: This framework opens multiple future directions for autonomous verification and maintenance of large-scale digital infrastructure. By demonstrating cost-effective RL training in real cloud environments, it enables continuous monitoring and automated documentation validation as platforms evolve—potentially reducing the estimated 4 million annual manual inspections to near-complete coverage. The high-determinism rollout system and reward modeling approach generalize to other complex, real-world agent tasks with sparse feedback and high execution costs. Future work could extend this to other platform types beyond cloud consoles, develop better methods for generalizing across rapidly changing UIs, and investigate how smaller distilled models can match frontier capabilities in other domains. The infrastructure patterns for stable RL in real, non-simulated environments provide foundations for agents in other high-cost or privacy-sensitive deployment contexts.
